tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text:
{"number":57104,"title":"Provisioning RegenSdkLocal script deletes tspconfig.yaml, losing emitter options","body":"## Problem

The provisioning \Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1\ script deletes \	spconfig.yaml\ from the fetched spec directory (line 175) to avoid linter rules that require packages not in the local \
ode_modules\. It then manually reconstructs emitter options via \--option\ flags.

This is inconsistent with the mgmt \Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1\, which keeps \	spconfig.yaml\ in place and lets \	sp compile\ read options naturally. The mgmt script only removes \package.json\.

## Impact

- Options set in \	spconfig.yaml\ (e.g. \pi-version\, \
amespace\, etc.) were silently dropped until a manual extraction workaround was added.
- Any new options added to the spec's \	spconfig.yaml\ for the provisioning emitter need to be explicitly forwarded in the script — easy to miss.

## Current Workaround

The script now reads all provisioning emitter options from \	spconfig.yaml\ before deleting it and forwards them as \--option\ flags. This works but is fragile.

## Ideal Fix

Align with the mgmt script approach: keep \	spconfig.yaml\ but strip the \linter\ section (or the \xtends\ within it) to avoid the linter dependency issue, rather than deleting the entire file.

## Related Files

- \ng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1\ (provisioning — deletes tspconfig)
- \ng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1\ (mgmt — keeps tspconfig)","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57104","user":{"login":"ArcturusZhang","id":10554446,"profile_url":"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4"},"author_association":"MEMBER","reactions":{"total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"created_at":"2026-03-14T12:04:29Z","updated_at":"2026-03-14T12:04:29Z"}

{"total_count":7,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4031739109,"number":56821,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Provisioning Migration - Kubernetes","body":"## Summary

Migrate `Azure.Provisioning.Kubernetes` from the reflection-based provisioning generator to the new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator (`http-client-csharp-provisioning`).

## Prerequisites

- The peer mgmt library `Azure.ResourceManager.Kubernetes` is already using the TypeSpec-based mgmt generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).

## Steps

1. Add `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to the TypeSpec spec with `emitterPackageJsonPath: "eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-provisioning-emitter-package.json"`
2. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` to generate the provisioning library
3. Compare generated output against existing hand-written library
4. Identify and track any issues found
5. Address issues and verify generated output matches expected API surface

## Related

- Provisioning generator PR: #56627
- AppConfiguration migration tracker: #56747

Migration - SignalR","body":"## Summary

Migrate `Azure.Provisioning.SignalR` from the reflection-based provisioning generator to the new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator (`http-client-csharp-provisioning`).

## Prerequisites

- The peer mgmt library `Azure.ResourceManager.SignalR` is already using the TypeSpec-based mgmt generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).

## Steps

1. Add `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to the TypeSpec spec with `emitterPackageJsonPath: "eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-provisioning-emitter-package.json"`
2. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` to generate the provisioning library
3. Compare generated output against existing hand-written library
4. Identify and track any issues found
5. Address issues and verify generated output matches expected API surface

## Related

- Provisioning generator PR: #56627
- AppConfiguration migration tracker: #56747

Migration - KeyVault","body":"## Summary

Migrate `Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault` from the reflection-based provisioning generator to the new TypeSpec-based provisioning generator (`http-client-csharp-provisioning`).

KeyVault is the primary test library for the provisioning generator because its peer mgmt library (`Azure.ResourceManager.KeyVault`) targets a stable GA API version (`2025-05-01`).

## Current Status

- **Generated and building** (0 compilation errors with `/p:RunApiCompat=false`)
- **214 unique ApiCompat breaking changes** (154 `CannotRemoveAttribute` for DataMember, 60 `MembersMustExist`/`TypesMustExist`)

## Issues Found

### Generator issues (shared with AppConfiguration)

| # | Issue | Severity | Status |
|---|-------|----------|--------|
| Mgmt `NameVisitor` property renames not applied (`CreationDate` -> `CreatedOn`, `ScheduledPurgeOn`) | Medium | Open - #56751 |
| Missing `[DataMember]` attribute on enum values | Medium | Open - #56752 (blocked by microsoft/typespec#9946) |
| Missing Roles / `KeyVaultBuiltInRole` | Medium | Open - #56744 |
| `ResourceVersions` should include all historical GA API versions | Info | Open - #56745 |
| Missing `GetResourceNameRequirements()` override | Low | Open - #56743 |
| Missing custom action methods | Low | Open - #56753 |

### KeyVault-specific observations

| Category | Count | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| `CannotRemoveAttribute` (DataMember) | 154 | Enum members missing `[DataMember]`  blocked by #56752 |
| `ResourceVersions` missing versions | 47 | Only `2025-05-01` generated, missing 10 historical versions  #56745 |
| `SecretAttributes` missing properties | 8 | `Enabled`, `Created`, `Expires`, `NotBefore`, `Updated`  model flattened differently |
| `BuiltInRole` / `CreateRoleAssignment` | 3 | `KeyVaultBuiltInRole` type not generated  #56744 |
| Date->On renames | 2 | `ScheduledPurgeOn`, `SecretAttributes.Created`  #56751 |
| `Tags` setter | 1 | `KeyVaultSecret.Tags` generated as read-only but was read-write |

## Prerequisites

- The peer mgmt library `Azure.ResourceManager.KeyVault` is already using the TypeSpec-based mgmt generator (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`).

## Related

- Provisioning generator PR: #56627
- AppConfiguration migration tracker: package","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ3ODYw"},{"id":6560170331,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Provisioning","name":"Provisioning","color":"ffeb77","description":"","default":false,"node_id":"LA_kwDOACyxMM8AAAABhwRBWw"}],"comments":0,"created_at":"2026-03-04T06:42:53Z","updated_at":"2026-03-04T16:30:52Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56738","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56738","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56738/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56738/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56738/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56738/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOACyxMM7volv1"},{"id":4015387699,"number":56708,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[Provisioning Generator] FlattenPropertyVisitor incorrectly flattens discriminator base types with few properties","body":"## Bug

When a discriminator base model type has only a single non-discriminator property, the `FlattenPropertyVisitor` in the mgmt generator marks it as `internal` (flattening it). This prevents users from working with the base type in polymorphic hierarchies.

### Example

Given a TypeSpec discriminated model:

```tsp
@discriminator("kind")
model BackupPolicy {
  kind: string;
  retentionDays?: int32;
}

model PeriodicBackupPolicy extends BackupPolicy {
  kind: "Periodic";
  intervalInHours: int32;
}
```

The generated `BackupPolicy` class is emitted as `internal` because after the discriminator property `kind` is excluded, it has only one property (`retentionDays`), triggering the `FlattenPropertyVisitor`'s flattening logic.

### Expected

Discriminator base types should remain `public` regardless of property count, since users need to reference them as the polymorphic base type.

### Actual

```csharp
internal partial class BackupPolicy : ProvisionableConstruct  // should be public
```

### Context

This was discovered during development of the Azure provisioning generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`).

Related PR: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56627

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** The `Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` script in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning` deletes `tspconfig.yaml` from the fetched spec directory to bypass linter dependency issues, then manually reconstructs emitter options via `--option` flags. This is fragile and inconsistent with the mgmt script approach (which keeps `tspconfig.yaml` and only removes `package.json`).

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Analysis</summary>

**Root Cause:**
The provisioning regen script removes the entire `tspconfig.yaml` to avoid linter rules that require packages not present in `node_modules`. As a result, any emitter options in `tspconfig.yaml` (e.g., `api-version`, `namespace`) are silently dropped unless the script explicitly re-forwards them as `--option` flags.

**Impact:**
- New emitter options added to a spec's `tspconfig.yaml` must be manually plumbed through the script — an easy-to-miss failure mode.
- The current workaround (reading all provisioning emitter options before deletion) works but is brittle and adds maintenance burden.

**Proposed Fix:**
Rather than deleting `tspconfig.yaml`, selectively strip the `linter` section (or the `$extends` within it) so linter-only dependencies are removed while all emitter options are preserved. This aligns with the mgmt script's pattern of only removing `package.json`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔧 Suggested Implementation Approach</summary>

1. In `Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` (provisioning), instead of deleting `tspconfig.yaml`:
   - Read the file as YAML/JSON
   - Remove the `linter` key (or just the `$extends` within it that pulls in the problematic package)
   - Write the modified content back to the file
2. Remove the manual option extraction / forwarding workaround
3. Verify the approach matches what `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` does for `tspconfig.yaml`

**Files to modify:**
- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` (line ~175)

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Related Issues</summary>

- [#56738 – Provisioning Generator tracking issue](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56738)
- [#56747 – AppConfiguration migration tracker](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56747)

</details>
